25: Of what do the elementals whisper?

26: What can you tell me of conjury?
Of what do the elementals whisper?

27: What can you tell me of conjury?

28: Sylphie oft catches the elementals' attention of late. The young conjurer attends to her studies with an impassioned vigor that surprises─and even inspires─her fellow initiates.

29: With such dedication and determination, it shall not be long before she earns the title of “Hearer.” Truly, your guidance has made of her a shining example for others to follow.
Sylphie oft catches the elementals' attention of late. The young conjurer attends to her studies with an impassioned vigor that surprises─and even inspires─her fellow initiates.

30: The rumblings of the forest speak of Good King Moggle Mog XII, and the mighty battle that saw him cast down from his throne.
With such dedication and determination, it shall not be long before she earns the title of “Hearer.” Truly, your guidance has made of her a shining example for others to follow.

31: Ever is the effort required to restore harmony greater than that expended in sowing chaos.
The rumblings of the forest speak of Good King Moggle Mog XII, and the mighty battle that saw him cast down from his throne.

32: When the nightmarish pall was lifted from the Lost City of Amdapor, the wind carried to me your name─the name of the Twelveswood's savior.
Ever is the effort required to restore harmony greater than that expended in sowing chaos.

33: Amdapor was not always thus afflicted. Indeed, the city once owed an age of prosperity to the benevolent art of white magic. But that was before the War of the Magi.
When the nightmarish pall was lifted from the Lost City of Amdapor, the wind carried to me your name─the name of the Twelveswood's savior.

34: It is told that the enemies of the Amdapori abandoned all caution in their pursuit of victory, and used their dark magicks to summon a king among demons. ...I imagine this abhorrent entity is the very evil you slew in the depths of the ruins.
Amdapor was not always thus afflicted. Indeed, the city once owed an age of prosperity to the benevolent art of white magic. But that was before the War of the Magi.

35: Though the mages of Amdapor eventually succeeded in imprisoning this fell creature, its recent resurgence speaks eloquently of the impermanence and unpredictability of arcane energies.

It is told that the enemies of the Amdapori abandoned all caution in their pursuit of victory, and used their dark magicks to summon a king among demons. ...I imagine this abhorrent entity is the very evil you slew in the depths of the ruins.

36: The elementals whisper of averted catastrophe. I understand that you worked alongside our conjurers in Garlemald, and A–Ruhn also sends word of your deeds.
Though the mages of Amdapor eventually succeeded in imprisoning this fell creature, its recent resurgence speaks eloquently of the impermanence and unpredictability of arcane energies.

37: 'Tis thanks to your bravery that our world is not now some barren husk. 'Tis thanks to your courage that we might continue living in peace in these woods.
The elementals whisper of averted catastrophe. I understand that you worked alongside our conjurers in Garlemald, and A–Ruhn also sends word of your deeds.

38: For two centuries and more have I walked this land, yet never have I cherished every stone and every branch as much as I do now. May the Matron and the great one bless you and keep you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>.
'Tis thanks to your bravery that our world is not now some barren husk. 'Tis thanks to your courage that we might continue living in peace in these woods.

39: What you have learned of our craft far exceeds aught I might explain in words. But perhaps from simple words you might gain new perspective.

For two centuries and more have I walked this land, yet never have I cherished every stone and every branch as much as I do now. May the Matron and the great one bless you and keep you, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>.

40: Conjury is an art that takes life and the living for its domain. Its primary purpose is the salving of hurts and the granting of protection.

41: Adventurers such as yourself oft stand upon the front lines of battle. Owing to this, you are like to find yourself in many situations wherein a capacity for healing would be advantageous. Mind you, there is more to conjury than that.

42: By harnessing the power of earth, wind, and water, conjurers are also capable of weaving spells that wreak havoc.
What you have learned of our craft far exceeds aught I might explain in words. But perhaps from simple words you might gain new perspective.

43: In terms of sheer destructive power, conjury may pale in comparison to thaumaturgy, but its capacity to defend one against aggression more than compensates for this relative shortcoming.
Conjury is an art that takes life and the living for its domain. Its primary purpose is the salving of hurts and the granting of protection.

44: In mastering healing and purification, not only will you be able to mend wounds and purge afflictions─you will also be able to breathe life back into the fallen.

Adventurers such as yourself oft stand upon the front lines of battle. Owing to this, you are like to find yourself in many situations wherein a capacity for healing would be advantageous. Mind you, there is more to conjury than that.

45: More than simply healers, yet not true dealers of destruction, conjurers realize their full potential when they employ their powers in support of others. This, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>, is what it means to be one of us.
By harnessing the power of earth, wind, and water, conjurers are also capable of weaving spells that wreak havoc.

46: I find it passing strange that an adventurer of your experience would require of me such an explanation. But we do oft gain new insight on the familiar when peering through another's eyes. Pray attend me, then, <Split(<Highlight>ObjectParameter(1)</Highlight>, ,1)/>.
In terms of sheer destructive power, conjury may pale in comparison to thaumaturgy, but its capacity to defend one against aggression more than compensates for this relative shortcoming.
